content: To prevent concrete cracking in cold weather, follow ACI 306 guidelines: (1) Maintain concrete temperature above 50°F for first 7 days using insulated blankets or heated enclosures. (2) Use low water-cement ratio (0.40-0.45) to reduce freezing risk. (3) Add air-entraining admixtures (4-7% air content) to provide freeze-thaw resistance. (4) Use calcium chloride or non-chloride accelerators to speed initial set, but limit CaCl2 to 2% by cement weight. (5) Avoid placing on frozen subgrade - thaw and maintain above freezing. (6) Extend curing time - concrete gains strength slower below 50°F. (7) Prevent thermal shock by gradual temperature reduction after protection period.
source: ACI 306R-16 Guide to Cold Weather Concreting & Portland Cement Association
